metadata.title: Build Handoff Conditions with Claude: Why 'Looks Good' Fails Every Time
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged

then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B09: palette=claude but the outro is 'NikBearBrownOutro' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: Looks good is a feeling. Feelings don't catch broken links that appear three days after the build.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Looks good is a feeling. Feelings don't catch broken links that appear three days after the build.

beats.narration_text: A handoff condition must be specific, testable, and binary. The weak versions all fail the binary test. The strong versions all have a bash one-liner that checks them.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: A handoff condition must be specific, testable, and binary. The weak versions all fail the binary test. The strong versions all have a bash one-liner that check

beats.narration_text: Ask for three strong handoff conditions: specific, testable, binary. Weak version versus strong version. Then a bash one-liner that checks all three.

props.command: claude "Write 3 strong handoff conditions for this build step: adding src/syllabus.html to a class website and linking it from nav. Each must be specific, testable, and binary. Show weak version vs. strong version. Then write a bash one-liner that checks all 3 automatically."

beats.narration_text: Read it before trusting it. Three conditions: HTTP 200, git diff shows only expected files, html-validate exits zero.

props.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Handoff check: syllabus.html added to class website
set -e

# Condition 1: file exists and returns HTTP 200
curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' ./src/syllabus.html \
  | grep -q 200 && echo 'C1 PASS' || echo 'C1 FAIL'

# Condition 2: only expected files modified
git diff --name-only HEAD | sort | \
  diff - <(printf 'src/index.html\nsrc/syllabus.html\n') \
  && echo 'C2 PASS' || echo 'C2 FAIL'

# Condition 3: html-validate passes
html-validate src/syllabus.html \
  && echo 'C3 PASS' || echo 'C3 FAIL'

beats.narration_text: Three conditions generated: weak versus strong for each. Bash check script runs green on the correct build: all three PASS. The script is the handoff — not the subjective review.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Three conditions generated: weak versus strong for each. Bash check script runs green on the correct build: all three PASS. The script is the handoff — not the

beats.narration_text: Deliberately introduce a broken nav link. Run the check script.

props.command:
# introduce broken nav link
sed -i 's/syllabus.html/syllabus-broken.html/' src/index.html
bash check_handoff.sh

beats.narration_text: With deliberate broken link in nav: check script exits 1. Condition 2 — git diff shows only expected files — FAIL: an unexpected file was modified. The script caught what a visual review would not have caught in three days of normal use.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: With deliberate broken link in nav: check script exits 1. Condition 2 — git diff shows only expected files — FAIL: an unexpected file was modified. The script c

beats.narration_text: Handoff conditions catch what you know to specify. The bash check script is the artifact — it makes the condition executable, not just declarative. The three properties — specific, testable, binary — are the rubric for evaluating any condition you write or that Claude generates for you.
